question.stem: A nonprofit volunteer group is prototyping a donation tracker and finds a bug that only appears with empty input. What is the best next testing action?
choices: Add boundary test cases including empty and minimum inputs
Assume users will not submit empty input
question.explanation: Boundary-focused testing catches edge-case failures early. A common wrong choice is "Test only average cases" because it sounds plausible but does not address the core condition. Takeaway: identify the exact requirement before selecting an approach.
